tool_input.description: End-to-end smoke test of train.py with tiny epoch/crop budget
Clean up temp test directory (no training run)

result:
## Summary

Implemented exp-55's dense per-cell voting + IRLS robust consensus design in `model/model.py` and `model/train.py`, per `runs/pending_experiment.json`'s brief.

**`model/model.py`**: deleted the pretrained MobileNetV3-Small trunk, `GRID_K` classification field, and all associated DSNT decode machinery. Replaced with a from-scratch 4-block conv trunk (24→48→64→96 channels, strides 2/2/2/1) producing a 16×16 feature grid; a 1×1-conv head regresses per-cell `(u, v, log_sigma)` for all 4 C4 rotations (1,024 votes/crop); a 3-round differentiable Geman-McClure IRLS solve produces the final consensus estimate; confidence comes from a small MLP over the final round's effective-sample-size and residual-spread stats, calibrated via the existing `conf_shift` mechanism.

**`model/train.py`**: replaced `compute_cell_weights` (1024-cell) with `compute_location_weights`, operating per individual training location (~45k for Berlin) — implemented with a chunked `|a-b|²=|a|²+|b|²-2a·b` nearest-neighbor search to keep the O(N²) search tractable (~34s one-time cost, verified on real Berlin data). Removed the trunk/head LR split (single Adam group, single LR). Updated the `loss_fn` call site and the SQLite-bound `info` dict fields accordingly.

**Verified** (without running actual training, per instructions): module imports cleanly, `backbonecheck.py` reports a clean `scratch` fingerprint, forward/backward pass works with correct shapes and non-zero gradients through the trunk and consensus/confidence heads, ONNX export succeeds (392 KB, well under the 4 MiB gate) with the exact `frame`→`uvc` contract, and host-CPU latency is ~4.5 ms (well under the 250 ms proxy budget).
